items_en: 1. Reaffirms that the staff of the United Nations is an irreplaceable resource, and welcomes its contribution to the realization of the purposes and principles of the United Nations;
2. Welcomes the memory of all staff members who have donated their lives to the service of the Organization;
3. Endorses the conclusions and recommendations contained in the reports of the Advisory Committee on Administrative and Budgetary Questions, subject to the provisions of the present resolution; 8

items_fr: 4. 8. Insiste sur le fait qu'il est crucial de sanctionner les rÃĐsultats insuffisants pour  que  les  mandats  soient  exÃĐcutÃĐs  avec  efficacitÃĐ  et  efficience,  et  demande instamment au SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral de clarifier et de simplifier les rÃĻgles et procÃĐdures rÃĐgissant la sanction des insuffisances, en gardant Ã  l'esprit les enseignements tirÃĐs de la jurisprudence issue du systÃĻme d'administration de la justice ;

9. Souligne l'importance  capitale  que  revÃŠt  la  rÃĐforme  de  la  gestion  des ressources humaines de l'Organisation, instrument de renforcement de l'efficacitÃĐ et de  l'efficience  de  l'Organisation  et  de  la  fonction  publique  internationale,  et rÃĐaffirme sa volontÃĐ de voir appliquÃĐe cette rÃĐforme ;
items_en: 4. Stresses that the principal organs of the United Nations have separate roles in personnel matters, and reaffirms that the Fifth Committee is the appropriate Main Committee of the General Assembly entrusted with responsibilities for administrative and budgetary matters, and that the Committee is responsible for analysing in depth and approving human, financial and related policies;

5. Welcomes the efforts of the Secretary-General to develop an enhanced performance management framework for the Organization, and requests him to submit to the General Assembly for its consideration at its sixty-ninth session a comprehensive report on performance management containing all the necessary proposals, modalities and recommendations;

6. Requests the Secretary-General to report on the progress made in the preparation of the comprehensive proposal on performance management at a briefing to be held no later than the second part of the resumed sixty-eighth session of the General Assembly;

7. Stresses that the main objective of the above-mentioned proposal should be to measure performance, reward good performance and sanction shortcomings in a credible and effective manner, and that it should be easily understood by staff and management;

8. Stresses the crucial importance of sanctioning underperformance for the effective and efficient implementation of mandates, and urges the Secretary-General to clarify and simplify the rules and procedures governing the sanctioning of inadequacies, bearing in mind the lessons learned from the jurisprudence of the system of administration of justice;

9. Stresses the critical importance of the reform of the human resources management of the Organization as an instrument for enhancing the efficiency and effectiveness of the Organization and the international civil service, and reaffirms its commitment to the implementation of that reform;

items_fr: 10. RÃĐaffirme son attachement Ã  l'intÃĐgritÃĐ et Ã  l'indÃĐpendance de la fonction publique internationale ;

items_en: 10. Reaffirms its commitment to the integrity and independence of the international civil service;
11. Urges the Secretary-General to ensure that any new proposals take into account the lessons learned from the implementation of previous reforms;

12. Requests the Secretary-General, in the context of human resources management reforms and operational transformation projects, to continue to ensure, in recruitment, the equal treatment of candidates with equivalent education, taking fully into account the fact that Member States have different education systems, none of which can be regarded as the Organization's reference model;

items_fr: 13. 18. Prie le  SecrÃĐtaire  gÃĐnÃĐral  de  concevoir  et  d'appliquer des  mesures permettant d'accÃĐlÃĐrer le recrutement Ã  partir de la liste des laurÃĐats du programme Jeunes administrateurs et de lui faire rapport sur les progrÃĻs accomplis sur ce plan Ã  sa soixante-neuviÃĻme session ;

19. Souligne le rÃīle important que le programme Jeunes administrateurs joue dans l'amÃĐlioration de la reprÃĐsentation des Ãtats Membres sous-reprÃĐsentÃĐs ou non reprÃĐsentÃĐs, prie le SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral de poursuivre les efforts qu'il dÃĐploie sur ce plan  et  note  que  certains  Ãtat  Membres  ne  comptent  pas  de  laurÃĐats  pour  les concours organisÃĐs dans le cadre de ce programme ;
items_en: 13. Recalls paragraph 5 of the report of the Advisory Committee, and requests the Secretary-General to include in the report on the composition of the Secretariat an analysis of demographic trends and factors that may explain them;

14. Also recalls paragraph 11 of the report of the Advisory Committee, and stresses the need for further efforts to improve the representation of women in the Secretariat, in accordance with the provisions of Article 101 of the Charter of the United Nations;

15. Stresses that one of the foundations of human resources management must be a comprehensive and robust system of forecasting personnel requirements, and notes with deep concern that the current staffing structure does not reflect the existence of such a system;

16. Urges the Secretary-General to develop, as a matter of priority, a staffing planning system and to submit it to the General Assembly for its consideration at its sixty-ninth session, and urges the Secretary-General to recommend changes to the staffing structure in accordance with the system to be adopted;

17. Recalls paragraph 7 of the report of the Advisory Committee, stresses the importance of a rejuvenation of the Secretariat, and requests the Secretary-General to keep population trends under constant review and to report thereon in his reports on the composition of the Secretariat;

18. Requests the Secretary-General to develop and implement measures to expedite recruitment from the roster of young professionals and to report on progress made in this regard to the General Assembly at its sixty-ninth session;

19. Stresses the important role played by the Young Professionals programme in improving the representation of underrepresented and unrepresented Member States, requests the Secretary-General to continue his efforts in this regard, and notes that some Member States do not have successful candidates in the competitive examinations under this programme;

text_fr: Pratique suivie par le SecrÃĐtaire gÃĐnÃĐral en matiÃĻre disciplinaire et dÃĐlictuelle

items_en: 20. Expresses its appreciation to the Secretary-General for the elimination of the backlog of disciplinary cases, and urges him to continue to monitor closely the handling of disciplinary cases, to intensify efforts to resolve them as soon as possible and to take additional steps to prevent further delays in the consideration of cases;
21. Invites the Secretary-General to provide further information in future reports on trends in the outcome of disciplinary appeals;

22. Encourages the Secretary-General to expeditiously finalize the new administrative instruction on investigations and disciplinary proceedings and to launch an information campaign to remind staff members of their responsibility to report misconduct and misconduct through appropriate channels;

23. Stresses the importance of taking all appropriate measures to reduce and recover losses resulting from misconduct by staff members, and requests the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly in this regard in his next report on human resources management;
